expectations.text: Each story uses a named persona, not 'a user'
expectations.evidence: All 5 stories use named personas: 'Marketing Analyst' (Stories 1-2), 'Sales Manager' (Story 3), 'Executive Stakeholder' (Stories 4-5). Note at top explains personas are inferred due to no product.local.md.

expectations.text: Each 'want' describes a capability (what the user can do), not a UI element (what they click)
expectations.evidence: Wants are capabilities: 'select a preset date range', 'select a custom start and end date', 'all dashboard widgets to update simultaneously', 'my selected date range to persist', 'see a clear error message'. None describe UI elements.

expectations.text: Each 'so that' describes a user outcome (benefit to them), not a system action
expectations.evidence: Outcomes are user-focused: 'quickly scope campaign metrics to standard reporting periods', 'analyse campaign performance for specific promotional windows', 'compare pipeline metrics across a consistent time window', 'do not have to re-select my reporting period every time I switch views', 'understand what to correct instead of seeing a broken or empty dashboard'.

expectations.text: No AC contains 'and' connecting multiple conditions
expectations.evidence: Reviewed all 20 ACs across 5 stories. Each AC tests a single condition-action-result. No compound 'and' statements found.

expectations.text: At least one error-state AC per story
expectations.evidence: Story 1 AC4 (data source error), Story 2 AC3/AC4/AC5 (invalid dates, future dates, missing fields), Story 3 AC3 (widget failure), Story 4 AC4 (invalid range overnight), Story 5 AC2/AC3 (unsupported format, 365-day limit).

expectations.text: Stories are sized (S/M/L) with dependencies noted
expectations.evidence: Story 1: M/None, Story 2: M/Story 1, Story 3: M/Story 1, Story 4: S/Story 1, Story 5: S/Story 2. All have Size and Dependencies fields.

expectations.text: Coverage check flags any spec AC not represented in stories
expectations.evidence: Coverage Analysis section maps all 5 spec ACs to stories (AC1→S1, AC2→S2, AC3→S3, AC4→S4, AC5→S5+S2). Also identifies 4 gaps: default range, URL shareability, timezone handling, accessibility.
